body:
```text
  ______           ____                 
 / ____/______  __/ __ )____ _________ 
/ /   / ___/ / / / __  / __ `/ ___/ _ \
/ /___/ /  / /_/ / /_/ / /_/ (__  )  __/
\____/_/   \__, /_____/\__,_/____/\___/ 
          /____/                         
```

# CryBase

[![Crystal](https://img.shields.io/badge/Crystal-1.20%2B-000000?logo=crystal&logoColor=white)](https://crystal-lang.org/)
[![Couchbase](https://img.shields.io/badge/Couchbase-KV%20Client-EA2328?logo=couchbase&logoColor=white)](https://www.couchbase.com/)

Crystal client primitives for Couchbase.

CryBase is still early, but it now has two useful layers:

- A cluster-level client that expands Couchbase connection strings into service
  endpoints and TCP-probes them.
- A KV client that speaks the Couchbase binary protocol for authenticated
  `get`, `set`, and `delete` operations, plus a fixed-size connection pool.

## Status

Implemented:

- Connection string parsing for `couchbase://`, `couchbases://`, and
  `http(s)://`.
- Service and endpoint modeling for KV, Query, Search, Analytics, Index,
  Eventing, Views, and Management.
- Plain TCP reachability probing for cluster service endpoints.
- KV binary protocol handshake: `HELLO`, SASL PLAIN auth, and `SELECT_BUCKET`.
- KV document operations: `get`, `set`, `delete`.
- Couchbase vbucket hashing for KV document routing.
- `KV::Pool` with 10 authenticated connections by default.
- Real Couchbase integration specs in GitHub Actions.

Not implemented yet:

- TLS socket handshake for KV operations.
- Cluster config loading and node/vbucket map routing.
- Retry, reconnect, durability, observe, CAS helpers, scopes, or collections.
- Query, Search, Analytics, Index, Eventing, Views, and Management protocols.

## Installation

Add CryBase to `shard.yml`:

```yaml
dependencies:
  crybase:
    github: num8er/crybase
```

Then install dependencies:

```sh
shards install
```

## Quick Start

### Probe Cluster Endpoints

```crystal
require "crybase"

client = CryBase::CouchBase::Client.connect("couchbase://127.0.0.1")

client.connect.each do |endpoint|
  puts endpoint
end

client.close
```

### Use One KV Connection

```crystal
require "crybase"

endpoint = CryBase::CouchBase::Endpoint.new(
  "127.0.0.1",
  11210,
  CryBase::CouchBase::Service::KV,
  false,
)

kv = CryBase::CouchBase::Services::KV::Client.new(
  endpoint,
  "Administrator",
  "password",
  "default",
)

kv.set("crybase:hello", %({"hello":"world"}))
puts String.new(kv.get("crybase:hello"))
kv.delete("crybase:hello")
kv.close
```

### Use The KV Pool

```crystal
require "crybase"

endpoint = CryBase::CouchBase::Endpoint.new(
  "127.0.0.1",
  11210,
  CryBase::CouchBase::Service::KV,
  false,
)

pool = CryBase::CouchBase::Services::KV::Pool.new(
  endpoint,
  "Administrator",
  "password",
  "default",
)

pool.set("crybase:pooled", "value")
puts String.new(pool.get("crybase:pooled"))

pool.checkout do |client|
  client.set("crybase:borrowed", "value")
end

pool.close
```

`KV::Pool` opens 10 connections by default. Override it with `size:`:

```crystal
pool = CryBase::CouchBase::Services::KV::Pool.new(
  endpoint,
  "Administrator",
  "password",
  "default",
  size: 20,
)
```

`KV::Client` and `KV::Pool` both expose `get`, `set`, `delete`, and `close`.
`KV::Pool` also exposes `checkout`, `closed?`, `size`, `endpoint`, and `bucket`.

## Public API Map

| Module / Type | Purpose |
| ------------- | ------- |
| `CryBase` | Top-level namespace and shard entry point. |
| `CryBase::CouchBase` | Couchbase-specific namespace. |
| `CryBase::CouchBase::ConnectionString` | Parses supported connection string schemes and seed hosts. |
| `CryBase::CouchBase::Endpoint` | Value type for one Couchbase service endpoint. |
| `CryBase::CouchBase::Service` | Service enum with plaintext and TLS default ports. |
| `CryBase::CouchBase::Client` | Cluster endpoint enumerator and TCP probe client. |
| `CryBase::CouchBase::Services` | Namespace for service-specific protocol clients. |
| `CryBase::CouchBase::Services::KV` | Couchbase binary KV protocol namespace. |
| `CryBase::CouchBase::Services::KV::Client` | Single authenticated KV connection. |
| `CryBase::CouchBase::Services::KV::Pool` | Fixed-size pool of authenticated KV clients. |
| `CryBase::Interfaces` | Abstract interface aliases for connection strings, endpoints, and clients. |

Generated API docs are committed in [`docs/`](docs/index.html).

## Connection Strings

| Scheme | TLS | Notes |
| ------ | --- | ----- |
| `couchbase://` | no | Plaintext service ports. Used by default if the scheme is omitted. |
| `couchbases://` | yes | TLS service ports. |
| `http://` | no | Treated as a Management URL. |
| `https://` | yes | Treated as a Management URL. |

Multiple seed nodes are comma-separated:

```text
couchbase://node1,node2,node3
```

An explicit `:port` is currently forwarded to the Management endpoint only.
Other services use their standard Couchbase ports.

## Service Ports

| Service | Plaintext | TLS |
| ------- | --------- | --- |
| Data (KV) | 11210 | 11207 |
| Query (N1QL) | 8093 | 18093 |
| Search (FTS) | 8094 | 18094 |
| Analytics | 8095 | 18095 |
| Index | 9102 | 19102 |
| Eventing | 8096 | 18096 |
| Views | 8092 | 18092 |
| Management | 8091 | 18091 |

## Examples

The `examples/` directory contains:

- `cluster_probe.cr` - probe reachable service endpoints.
- `kv_basics.cr` - run a basic KV set/get flow against one endpoint.
- `kv_endpoint_from_cluster.cr` - probe the cluster, pick a KV endpoint, and
  run a KV operation.
- `docker-compose.yml` - local Couchbase Community setup for development.

The examples read Couchbase settings from environment variables:

```sh
export COUCHBASE_HOST=127.0.0.1
export COUCHBASE_USER=Administrator
export COUCHBASE_PASS=password
export COUCHBASE_BUCKET=default
```

## Development

Run checks:

```sh
crystal tool format --check
crystal build --no-codegen src/crybase.cr
crystal spec --error-trace
```

Generate API docs:

```sh
crystal docs -o docs
```

Run real Couchbase integration specs:

```sh
COUCHBASE_INTEGRATION=1 crystal spec spec/integration --error-trace
```

Enable local hooks once per clone:

```sh
git config core.hooksPath .githooks
```

The pre-commit hook:

- Checks Crystal formatting.
- Verifies the library builds.
- Regenerates `docs/`.
- Fails if regenerated docs are not staged.
- Runs the spec suite.

## GitHub Actions

CI runs:

- Unit specs.
- Formatting.
- Real Couchbase integration specs against `couchbase:community-7.6.0`.

## Project Conventions

- One flat module per file, for example `module CryBase::CouchBase`.
- Folder paths mirror namespaces.
- Every `class`, `struct`, and `record` has its own file.
- Prefer small value types and explicit protocol framing.
- Keep comments focused on non-obvious behavior.

## Contributing

1. Fork the repository.
2. Create a feature branch.
3. Run format, build, specs, and docs generation.
4. Commit using Conventional Commits.
5. Open a pull request.

## Contributors

- [Anar K. Jafarov](https://github.com/num8er) - creator and maintainer

types.name: CryBase

constants.name: VERSION
constants.doc:
Shard version, kept in sync with `shard.yml`.

```
CryBase::VERSION # => "0.0.1"
```

types.doc:
Top-level namespace for the CryBase database client toolkit.

Each supported database lives in its own sub-namespace under `CryBase`,
implementing the abstractions defined in `CryBase::Interfaces`.
Currently shipped backend: `CryBase::CouchBase`.

```
require "crybase"

client = CryBase::CouchBase::Client.new("couchbase://localhost")
client.connect
```
types.summary: Top-level namespace for the CryBase database client toolkit.

types.name: CouchBase
types.doc:
Couchbase Server adapter for CryBase.

Maps the cluster-wide concepts — connection strings, services (KV/Query/…),
endpoints, and a multi-service client — onto the abstract types declared
in `CryBase::Interfaces`.

```
require "crybase"

client = CryBase::CouchBase::Client.connect("couchbase://node1,node2")
kv_endpoint = client.endpoints_for(CryBase::CouchBase::Service::KV).first
client.close
```

For protocol-level work against a specific service, see
`CryBase::CouchBase::Services` (e.g. `Services::KV::Client`).
types.summary: Couchbase Server adapter for CryBase.

types.name: Client
types.doc:
Cluster-level client that enumerates and probes every Couchbase
service interface (KV, Query, Search, Analytics, Index, Eventing,
Views, Management) for the configured hosts, over either plaintext
or TLS ports.

No protocol handshake is performed at this layer — `connect` only
validates TCP reachability so callers can confirm the cluster's
network surface before driving a service-specific protocol (for
example `CryBase::CouchBase::Services::KV::Client`).

```
client = CryBase::CouchBase::Client.connect("couchbase://node1,node2")
kv_eps = client.endpoints_for(CryBase::CouchBase::Service::KV)
client.close
```
types.summary: Cluster-level client that enumerates and probes every Couchbase service interface (KV, Query, Search, Analytics, Index, Eventing, Views, Management) for the configured hosts, over either plaintext or TLS ports.

constructors.name: connect
constructors.doc:
Builds the client and immediately calls `connect`. Equivalent to
`new(...).tap(&.connect)`.

```
client = CryBase::CouchBase::Client.connect(
  "couchbases://node1,node2",
  username: "Administrator",
  password: "s3cret",
)
```
constructors.args_string: (uri : String, username : String | Nil = nil, password : String | Nil = nil, connect_timeout : Time::Span = 5.seconds) : Client
constructors.args_html: (uri : String, username : String | Nil = nil, password : String | Nil = nil, connect_timeout : Time::Span = 5.seconds) : Client

def.name: connect
def.body:
client = new(uri, username, password, connect_timeout)
client.connect
client

constructors.name: new
constructors.doc:
Parses *uri* and pre-computes the full `endpoints` matrix
(one per host × service). No network activity yet.
constructors.summary: Parses uri and pre-computes the full #endpoints matrix (one per host × service).
constructors.args_string: (uri : String, username : String | Nil = nil, password : String | Nil = nil, connect_timeout : Time::Span = 5.seconds)
constructors.args_html: (uri : String, username : String | Nil = nil, password : String | Nil = nil, connect_timeout : Time::Span = 5.seconds)

def.name: new
def.body:
_ = allocate
_.initialize(uri, username, password, connect_timeout)
if _.responds_to?(:finalize)
  ::GC.add_finalizer(_)
end
_

instance_methods.name: close
instance_methods.doc:
Marks the client as no longer `connected?`. This layer holds no
persistent sockets, so this is purely a state flip.

instance_methods.name: connect
instance_methods.doc:
Probes every endpoint over TCP and returns the reachable subset.
Marks the client as `connected?` if at least one endpoint
responded; otherwise raises `IO::Error`.

```
reachable = client.connect # => [Endpoint, Endpoint, ...]
```
instance_methods.summary: Probes every endpoint over TCP and returns the reachable subset.

def.name: connect
def.body:
reachable = [] of Endpoint
@endpoints.each do |ep|
  if probe(ep)
    reachable << ep
  end
end

if reachable.empty?
  raise(IO::Error.new("no Couchbase service endpoints reachable for hosts #{@connection_string.hosts.join(", ")}"))
end

@connected = true
reachable

instance_methods.name: connected?
instance_methods.doc: Whether the client has completed a successful `connect`.
instance_methods.summary: Whether the client has completed a successful #connect.

instance_methods.name: connection_string
instance_methods.doc: The parsed connection string the client was constructed from.
instance_methods.summary: The parsed connection string the client was constructed from.

instance_methods.name: endpoints_for
instance_methods.doc:
Returns every `Endpoint` for the given *service*, in host order.

```
client.endpoints_for(CryBase::CouchBase::Service::KV).map(&.host)
# => ["node1", "node2"]
```
instance_methods.summary: Returns every Endpoint for the given service, in host order.

types.name: ConnectionString
types.doc:
Parses a Couchbase connection string of the form:

* `couchbase://host[,host2][:port]`     — plaintext
* `couchbases://host[,host2][:port]`    — TLS
* `http(s)://host[:port]`               — treated as the Management URL

```
cs = CryBase::CouchBase::ConnectionString.parse("couchbases://n1,n2:18091")
cs.hosts         # => ["n1", "n2"]
cs.tls           # => true
cs.explicit_port # => 18091
```
types.summary: Parses a Couchbase connection string of the form:

constructors.name: new
constructors.args_string: (hosts : Array(String), tls : Bool, explicit_port : Int32 | Nil = nil)
constructors.args_html: (hosts : Array(String), tls : Bool, explicit_port : Int32 | Nil = nil)

constructors.name: parse
constructors.doc:
Parses *input* into a `ConnectionString`.

Raises `ArgumentError` if the string is empty or has no host
component.

```
ConnectionString.parse("localhost")          # plaintext, no port
ConnectionString.parse("couchbases://h1,h2") # TLS, two hosts
```

def.name: parse
def.body:
if input.empty?
  raise(ArgumentError.new("connection string is empty"))
end

scheme, rest = split_scheme(input)
tls = scheme.in?({"couchbases", "https"})

hosts_part, port = split_host_port(rest)
hosts = (hosts_part.split(',', remove_empty: true)).map(&.strip)
if hosts.empty?
  raise(ArgumentError.new("no hosts parsed from #{input.inspect}"))
end

new(hosts, tls, port)

instance_methods.name: explicit_port
instance_methods.doc:
Port explicitly given in the URI, or `nil` when none was supplied.
Used to override the `Management` service's default port; other
services always use their `Service#default_port`.
instance_methods.summary: Port explicitly given in the URI, or nil when none was supplied.

instance_methods.name: hosts
instance_methods.doc: The cluster nodes parsed from the connection string.
instance_methods.summary: The cluster nodes parsed from the connection string.

instance_methods.name: tls
instance_methods.doc: Whether the connection should be made over TLS.
instance_methods.summary: Whether the connection should be made over TLS.

types.name: Endpoint
types.doc:
A single addressable Couchbase service interface — the combination
of a host, a port, the `Service` running on that port, and whether
the connection should be TLS.

```
ep = CryBase::CouchBase::Endpoint.new("h1", 11210, CryBase::CouchBase::Service::KV, false)
ep.scheme  # => "couchbase"
ep.address # => "couchbase://h1:11210"
ep.to_s    # => "Data (KV) couchbase://h1:11210"
```
types.summary: A single addressable Couchbase service interface — the combination of a host, a port, the Service running on that port, and whether the connection should be TLS.

constructors.name: new
constructors.args_string: (host : String, port : Int32, service : Service, tls : Bool)
constructors.args_html: (host : String, port : Int32, service : Service, tls : Bool)

def.name: new
def.body:
_ = allocate
_.initialize(host, port, service, tls)
if _.responds_to?(:finalize)
  ::GC.add_finalizer(_)
end
_

instance_methods.name: address
instance_methods.doc:
Full `scheme://host:port` string for this endpoint.

```
Endpoint.new("h", 11210, Service::KV, false).address # => "couchbase://h:11210"
```

instance_methods.name: host
instance_methods.doc: The hostname or IP address for this endpoint.
instance_methods.summary: The hostname or IP address for this endpoint.

instance_methods.name: port
instance_methods.doc: The TCP port for this endpoint.

instance_methods.name: scheme
instance_methods.doc:
The URI scheme appropriate for this endpoint:
* `"couchbase"` / `"couchbases"` for the KV service
* `"http"` / `"https"` for every other service

```
Endpoint.new("h", 11210, Service::KV, false).scheme   # => "couchbase"
Endpoint.new("h", 11207, Service::KV, true).scheme    # => "couchbases"
Endpoint.new("h", 8093, Service::Query, false).scheme # => "http"
```
instance_methods.summary: The URI scheme appropriate for this endpoint: * "couchbase" / "couchbases" for the KV service * "http" / "https" for every other service

def.name: scheme
def.body:
case service
when .kv?
  tls ? "couchbases" : "couchbase"
else
  tls ? "https" : "http"
end

instance_methods.name: tls
instance_methods.doc: Whether this endpoint speaks TLS.

instance_methods.name: to_s
instance_methods.doc:
Renders the endpoint as `"<service display name> <address>"`.

```
Endpoint.new("h", 11210, Service::KV, false).to_s # => "Data (KV) couchbase://h:11210"
```

types.name: Service
types.doc:
Catalog of services a Couchbase node can expose. Couchbase's
multi-dimensional scaling lets each node enable any subset of these,
and each service runs on its own well-known port.

```
CryBase::CouchBase::Service::KV.default_port(false) # => 11210
CryBase::CouchBase::Service::KV.default_port(true)  # => 11207
CryBase::CouchBase::Service::Query.display_name     # => "Query (N1QL)"
```
types.summary: Catalog of services a Couchbase node can expose.

instance_methods.name: analytics?
instance_methods.doc: Returns `true` if this enum value equals `Analytics`

instance_methods.name: default_port
instance_methods.doc:
Returns the well-known port this service listens on for the given
transport — `tls=true` for the TLS variant, `tls=false` for plaintext.

```
Service::Management.default_port(false) # => 8091
Service::Management.default_port(true)  # => 18091
```
instance_methods.summary: Returns the well-known port this service listens on for the given transport — tls=true for the TLS variant, tls=false for plaintext.

def.name: default_port
def.body:
case self
in KV
  tls ? 11207 : 11210
in Query
  tls ? 18093 : 8093
in Search
  tls ? 18094 : 8094
in Analytics
  tls ? 18095 : 8095
in Eventing
  tls ? 18096 : 8096
in Views
  tls ? 18092 : 8092
in Index
  tls ? 19102 : 9102
in Management
  tls ? 18091 : 8091
end

instance_methods.name: display_name
instance_methods.doc:
Human-readable name as it appears in Couchbase's own UI/docs
(e.g. `"Data (KV)"`, `"Query (N1QL)"`).

```
Service::KV.display_name     # => "Data (KV)"
Service::Search.display_name # => "Search (FTS)"
```
instance_methods.summary: Human-readable name as it appears in Couchbase's own UI/docs (e.g.

def.name: display_name
def.body:
case self
in KV
  "Data (KV)"
in Query
  "Query (N1QL)"
in Search
  "Search (FTS)"
in Analytics
  "Analytics"
in Index
  "Index"
in Eventing
  "Eventing"
in Views
  "Views"
in Management
  "Management"
end

instance_methods.doc: Returns `true` if this enum value equals `Eventing`

instance_methods.name: index?
instance_methods.doc: Returns `true` if this enum value equals `Index`

instance_methods.name: kv?
instance_methods.doc: Returns `true` if this enum value equals `KV`

types.name: Services
types.doc:
Namespace for protocol-level implementations of individual Couchbase
services. The cluster-level `CryBase::CouchBase::Client` only validates
TCP reachability; types under `Services` speak each service's actual
wire protocol.

Currently implemented:
* `Services::KV` — memcached binary protocol against the KV service

```
require "crybase"

CryBase::CouchBase::Services.list.size # => 8

endpoint = CryBase::CouchBase::Endpoint.new(
  "node1", 11210, CryBase::CouchBase::Service::KV, false
)
kv = CryBase::CouchBase::Services::KV::Client.new(
  endpoint, "user", "pass", "default"
)
```
types.summary: Namespace for protocol-level implementations of individual Couchbase services.

class_methods.name: list
class_methods.doc:
Returns every member of the `CryBase::CouchBase::Service` enum, in
the canonical order used when building the per-host endpoint matrix.

```
CryBase::CouchBase::Services.list
# => [Service::KV, Service::Query, Service::Search, Service::Analytics,
#     Service::Index, Service::Eventing, Service::Views, Service::Management]
```
class_methods.summary: Returns every member of the CryBase::CouchBase::Service enum, in the canonical order used when building the per-host endpoint matrix.

def.name: list
def.body: [Service::KV, Service::Query, Service::Search, Service::Analytics, Service::Index, Service::Eventing, Service::Views, Service::Management]

types.name: KV

constants.name: AGENT
constants.doc: User-agent string sent in the HELLO request body.
constants.summary: User-agent string sent in the HELLO request body.

constants.name: FEATURE_SELECT_BUCKET
constants.doc:
HELLO feature code that opts the connection into bucket selection.
Sent during the handshake so the server accepts SELECT_BUCKET.
constants.summary: HELLO feature code that opts the connection into bucket selection.

constants.name: HEADER_SIZE
constants.doc: Fixed-width header size in bytes for both requests and responses.
constants.summary: Fixed-width header size in bytes for both requests and responses.

constants.name: REQUEST_MAGIC
constants.doc: Binary-protocol magic byte indicating an outbound (request) packet.
constants.summary: Binary-protocol magic byte indicating an outbound (request) packet.

constants.name: RESPONSE_MAGIC
constants.doc: Binary-protocol magic byte indicating an inbound (response) packet.
constants.summary: Binary-protocol magic byte indicating an inbound (response) packet.

constants.name: VBUCKET_COUNT
constants.doc: Number of vbuckets in Couchbase buckets.

types.doc:
Implementation of the Couchbase KV (Data) service over the memcached
binary protocol.

The public entry point is `KV::Client`; everything else is structured
as small composable pieces:

* `KV::Request`        — value type describing one outbound packet
* `KV::RequestWriter`  — mixin: `write(req)` serializes to a socket
* `KV::Response`       — value type describing one inbound packet
* `KV::ResponseReader` — mixin: `read` decodes one packet from a socket
* `KV::Bucket`         — mixin: SELECT_BUCKET handshake
* `KV::Pool`           — fixed-size pool of authenticated clients

These mixins are composed into `KV::Client`, which also handles
`HELLO`, `SASL_AUTH(PLAIN)` and offers `get`/`set`/`delete`.

```
endpoint = CryBase::CouchBase::Endpoint.new(
  "node1", 11210, CryBase::CouchBase::Service::KV, false
)
kv = CryBase::CouchBase::Services::KV::Client.new(endpoint, "user", "pass", "default")
kv.set("hello", "world")
kv.get("hello") # => Bytes containing "world"
kv.delete("hello")
kv.close
```
types.summary: Implementation of the Couchbase KV (Data) service over the memcached binary protocol.

types.name: AuthFailed
types.doc:
Raised when the server returns `Status::AuthError` or
`Status::AuthContinue`. Typical source: bad SASL credentials, or
a SELECT_BUCKET against a bucket the user can't access.

```
begin
  KV::Client.new(endpoint, "user", "wrong-pass", "default")
rescue KV::AuthFailed
  # bad creds
end
```
types.summary: Raised when the server returns Status::AuthError or Status::AuthContinue.

types.name: Bucket
types.doc:
Mixin that performs the SELECT_BUCKET handshake — switches the
connection's active bucket. Depends on the includer also mixing
in `RequestWriter` (for `write`) and `ResponseReader` (for `read`).

Maps server-side errors onto typed exceptions:
`Status::AuthError`/`AuthContinue` → `AuthFailed`, anything else
non-success → plain `Error`.

```
class Peer
  include KV::RequestWriter
  include KV::ResponseReader
  include KV::Bucket

  def initialize(@socket : IO)
  end
end

# `io` is wired to a real KV node (or a fake responder)
Peer.new(io).use("default")
```
types.summary: Mixin that performs the SELECT_BUCKET handshake — switches the connection's active bucket.

types.name: Client
types.doc:
Talks the Couchbase memcached binary protocol against a single KV
node. Composed from `RequestWriter`, `ResponseReader`, and `Bucket`
mixins.

On construct: TCP-connects to *endpoint*, sends `HELLO` (advertising
`FEATURE_SELECT_BUCKET`), `SASL_AUTH` (PLAIN), and `SELECT_BUCKET` for
*bucket*. Then exposes plain `get` / `set` / `delete` against that
bucket.

```
endpoint = CryBase::CouchBase::Endpoint.new(
  "node1", 11210, CryBase::CouchBase::Service::KV, false
)
kv = KV::Client.new(endpoint, "user", "pass", "default")
kv.set("hello", "world")
kv.get("hello") # => "world".to_slice
kv.delete("hello")
kv.close
```

Out of scope (deliberate, can be layered on top later):
* CAS, flags, durability, observe, and other op modifiers
* reconnect / retry
types.summary: Talks the Couchbase memcached binary protocol against a single KV node.

constructors.name: new
constructors.doc:
Connects, performs HELLO + SASL PLAIN + SELECT_BUCKET, and leaves
the client ready for `get`/`set`/`delete`.

Raises:
* `IO::Error` / `Socket::Error` — TCP connect failed
* `KV::AuthFailed` — SASL auth or bucket selection denied
* `KV::Error` — server returned any other non-success status

The socket is closed and the exception re-raised if any handshake
step fails.
constructors.summary: Connects, performs HELLO + SASL PLAIN + SELECT_BUCKET, and leaves the client ready for #get/#set/#delete.
constructors.args_string: (endpoint : Endpoint, username : String, password : String, bucket : String, connect_timeout : Time::Span = 5.seconds)
constructors.args_html: (endpoint : Endpoint, username : String, password : String, bucket : String, connect_timeout : Time::Span = 5.seconds)

def.name: new
def.body:
_ = allocate
_.initialize(endpoint, username, password, bucket, connect_timeout)
if _.responds_to?(:finalize)
  ::GC.add_finalizer(_)
end
_

instance_methods.name: bucket
instance_methods.doc: The bucket selected during the construction handshake.
instance_methods.summary: The bucket selected during the construction handshake.

instance_methods.name: close
instance_methods.doc:
Closes the underlying TCP socket. Idempotent — safe to call when
already closed.

instance_methods.name: delete
instance_methods.doc:
Deletes the document at *key*. Raises `NotFound` if absent.

```
kv.delete("hello")
```

def.name: delete
def.body:
resp = call(Opcode::Delete, key: key, vbucket: vbucket_id(key))
ensure_success!(resp, "DELETE #{key}")

instance_methods.name: endpoint
instance_methods.doc: The `Endpoint` this client is connected to.

instance_methods.name: get
instance_methods.doc:
Fetches the document at *key*. Raises `NotFound` if absent.

```
bytes = kv.get("user:42")
JSON.parse(String.new(bytes))
```

def.name: get
def.body:
resp = call(Opcode::Get, key: key, vbucket: vbucket_id(key))
ensure_success!(resp, "GET #{key}")
resp.value

instance_methods.name: set
instance_methods.doc:
Stores *value* at *key* with optional *expiry* (seconds, or unix
timestamp if greater than 30 days). Returns the new CAS token.

```
cas = kv.set("hello", "world")
cas = kv.set("hello", "world".to_slice, expiry: 60_u32)
```
instance_methods.summary: Stores value at key with optional expiry (seconds, or unix timestamp if greater than 30 days).
instance_methods.args_string: (key : String, value : String | Bytes, expiry : UInt32 = 0_u32) : UInt64
instance_methods.args_html: (key : String, value : String | Bytes, expiry : UInt32 = 0_u32) : UInt64

def.name: set
def.body:
bytes = value.is_a?(String) ? value.to_slice : value
extras = IO::Memory.new(8)
extras.write_bytes(0_u32, IO::ByteFormat::BigEndian)
extras.write_bytes(expiry, IO::ByteFormat::BigEndian)
resp = call(Opcode::Set, key: key, extras: extras.to_slice, value: bytes, vbucket: vbucket_id(key))
ensure_success!(resp, "SET #{key}")
resp.cas

types.name: Error
types.doc:
Base exception for any non-`Success` status returned by the KV
service. Subclasses (`NotFound`, `AuthFailed`) cover well-known
cases; everything else surfaces as a plain `Error`.

```
begin
  kv.get("missing")
rescue ex : KV::Error
  ex.status # => KV::Status::KeyNotFound
end
```
types.summary: Base exception for any non-Success status returned by the KV service.

instance_methods.name: status
instance_methods.doc: The protocol status that triggered the exception.
instance_methods.summary: The protocol status that triggered the exception.

types.name: NotFound
types.doc:
Raised when the server replies with `Status::KeyNotFound`. Typical
source: `Client#get` for an absent key.

```
begin
  kv.get("never-set")
rescue KV::NotFound
  # handle missing key
end
```
types.summary: Raised when the server replies with Status::KeyNotFound.

types.name: Opcode
types.doc:
Operation codes for the memcached binary protocol commands this
client speaks. Values match the Couchbase Server wire protocol
specification.

```
KV::Opcode::Get.value          # => 0x00
KV::Opcode::SelectBucket.value # => 0x89
```
types.summary: Operation codes for the memcached binary protocol commands this client speaks.

instance_methods.name: delete?
instance_methods.doc: Returns `true` if this enum value equals `Delete`

instance_methods.name: get?
instance_methods.doc: Returns `true` if this enum value equals `Get`

instance_methods.name: hello?
instance_methods.doc: Returns `true` if this enum value equals `Hello`

instance_methods.name: sasl_auth?
instance_methods.doc: Returns `true` if this enum value equals `SaslAuth`

instance_methods.name: select_bucket?
instance_methods.doc: Returns `true` if this enum value equals `SelectBucket`

instance_methods.name: set?
instance_methods.doc: Returns `true` if this enum value equals `Set`

types.name: Pool
types.doc: Fixed-size pool of authenticated KV clients for concurrent fibers.
types.summary: Fixed-size pool of authenticated KV clients for concurrent fibers.

constructors.name: new
constructors.args_string: (endpoint : Endpoint, username : String, password : String, bucket : String, size : Int32 = DEFAULT_SIZE, connect_timeout : Time::Span = 5.seconds)
constructors.args_html: (endpoint : Endpoint, username : String, password : String, bucket : String, size : Int32 = DEFAULT_SIZE, connect_timeout : Time::Span = 5.seconds)

def.name: new
def.body:
_ = allocate
_.initialize(endpoint, username, password, bucket, size, connect_timeout)
if _.responds_to?(:finalize)
  ::GC.add_finalizer(_)
end
_

def.name: checkout
def.body:
if closed?
  raise_closed!
end

client = @available.receive
if closed?
  client.close
  raise_closed!
end

begin
  yield client
ensure
  if closed?
    client.close
  else
    @available.send(client)
  end
end

def.name: close
def.body:
should_close = @mutex.synchronize do
  if @closed
    next false
  end

  @closed = true
  true
end

if should_close
else
  return
end
@clients.each(&.close)

instance_methods.name: set
instance_methods.args_string: (key : String, value : String | Bytes, expiry : UInt32 = 0_u32) : UInt64
instance_methods.args_html: (key : String, value : String | Bytes, expiry : UInt32 = 0_u32) : UInt64

def.name: set
def.body: checkout do |client| client.set(key, value, expiry) end

types.name: Request
types.doc:
One outbound packet for the KV service, as a pure value type.
`RequestWriter#write` knows how to serialize it.

```
req = KV::Request.new(KV::Opcode::Get, key: "hello")
req.opcode # => KV::Opcode::Get
req.key    # => "hello"
req.opaque # => 0_u32
```

The fields:

* *opcode*  — the command code (`Opcode::Get`, `::Set`, etc.)
* *key*     — the document/operation key (UTF-8 string)
* *extras*  — opcode-specific bytes that precede the body
* *value*   — the document/operation body
* *cas*     — compare-and-swap token, or `0` for "any"
* *opaque*  — caller-controlled echo field; the server returns it
              verbatim in the response
* *vbucket* — key partition id used by document operations
types.summary: One outbound packet for the KV service, as a pure value type.

constructors.name: new
constructors.args_string: (opcode : Opcode, key : String = "", extras : Bytes = Bytes.empty, value : Bytes = Bytes.empty, cas : UInt64 = 0_u64, opaque : UInt32 = 0_u32, vbucket : UInt16 = 0_u16)
constructors.args_html: (opcode : Opcode, key : String = "", extras : Bytes = Bytes.empty, value : Bytes = Bytes.empty, cas : UInt64 = 0_u64, opaque : UInt32 = 0_u32, vbucket : UInt16 = 0_u16)

def.name: new
def.body:
_ = allocate
_.initialize(opcode, key, extras, value, cas, opaque, vbucket)
if _.responds_to?(:finalize)
  ::GC.add_finalizer(_)
end
_

instance_methods.name: copy_with
instance_methods.args_string: (opcode _opcode = @opcode, key _key = @key, extras _extras = @extras, value _value = @value, cas _cas = @cas, opaque _opaque = @opaque, vbucket _vbucket = @vbucket)
instance_methods.args_html: (opcode _opcode = @opcode, key _key = @key, extras _extras = @extras, value _value = @value, cas _cas = @cas, opaque _opaque = @opaque, vbucket _vbucket = @vbucket)

types.name: RequestWriter
types.doc:
Mixin that serializes a `Request` into the binary KV wire format
and writes it to the includer's `@socket`. The includer is expected
to own an IO-typed `@socket` (e.g. `TCPSocket` in `KV::Client`).

Used together with `ResponseReader` and `Bucket` to compose
`KV::Client`. Stateless on its own.

```
class Peer
  include KV::RequestWriter

  def initialize(@socket : IO)
  end
end

io = IO::Memory.new
Peer.new(io).write(KV::Request.new(KV::Opcode::Get, key: "foo"))
```
types.summary: Mixin that serializes a Request into the binary KV wire format and writes it to the includer's @socket.

types.name: Response
types.doc:
One decoded inbound packet from the KV service. Produced by
`ResponseReader#read`.

```
resp = KV::Response.new(
  opcode: KV::Opcode::Get.value,
  status: KV::Status::Success,
  cas: 0_u64,
  extras: Bytes.empty,
  key: "",
  value: "world".to_slice,
)
resp.success?          # => true
String.new(resp.value) # => "world"
```
types.summary: One decoded inbound packet from the KV service.

constructors.name: new
constructors.args_string: (opcode : UInt8, status : Status, cas : UInt64, extras : Bytes, key : String, value : Bytes)
constructors.args_html: (opcode : UInt8, status : Status, cas : UInt64, extras : Bytes, key : String, value : Bytes)

def.name: new
def.body:
_ = allocate
_.initialize(opcode, status, cas, extras, key, value)
if _.responds_to?(:finalize)
  ::GC.add_finalizer(_)
end
_

instance_methods.name: success?
instance_methods.doc: `true` iff `status == Status::Success`.

types.name: ResponseReader
types.doc:
Mixin that reads exactly one `Response` packet from the includer's
`@socket`. The includer is expected to own an IO-typed `@socket`.

Used together with `RequestWriter` and `Bucket` to compose
`KV::Client`. Stateless on its own.

```
class Peer
  include KV::ResponseReader

  def initialize(@socket : IO)
  end
end

# `io` already contains a server response packet, positioned at start.
resp = Peer.new(io).read
```
types.summary: Mixin that reads exactly one Response packet from the includer's @socket.

types.name: Status
types.doc:
Status codes returned by the server in every response packet.
`Success` (`0x0000`) is the only non-error value. The `Response`
struct keeps the typed status; `Client` maps a few of these onto
specific exception subclasses.

```
KV::Status::Success.value     # => 0x0000
KV::Status::KeyNotFound.value # => 0x0001
```
types.summary: Status codes returned by the server in every response packet.

instance_methods.doc: Returns `true` if this enum value equals `NoBucket`

instance_methods.doc: Returns `true` if this enum value equals `NotMyVbucket`

instance_methods.doc: Returns `true` if this enum value equals `NotSupported`

instance_methods.name: out_of_memory?
instance_methods.doc: Returns `true` if this enum value equals `OutOfMemory`

types.name: Interfaces
types.doc:
Database-agnostic abstract types every backend in CryBase implements.

Each abstract here defines the minimum contract a concrete adapter
(`CryBase::CouchBase::*`, future `CryBase::Postgres::*`, …) must satisfy:

* `Interfaces::ConnectionString` — parsed cluster/database address
* `Interfaces::Endpoint`         — single addressable network target
* `Interfaces::Client`           — connection-bearing entry point

```
def connect(uri : String) : CryBase::Interfaces::Client
  CryBase::CouchBase::Client.connect(uri)
end
```
types.summary: Database-agnostic abstract types every backend in CryBase implements.

types.name: Client
types.doc:
Common contract every database-specific client in CryBase must satisfy.
Subtypes carry their own auth, endpoint shape, and `connect` semantics
on top of this base.

```
client = CryBase::CouchBase::Client.new("couchbase://localhost")
client.is_a?(CryBase::Interfaces::Client) # => true
client.connection_string.hosts            # => ["localhost"]
client.connected?                         # => false
client.close
```
types.summary: Common contract every database-specific client in CryBase must satisfy.

instance_methods.name: close
instance_methods.doc: Releases any resources held by the client. Must be idempotent.
instance_methods.summary: Releases any resources held by the client.

instance_methods.name: connected?
instance_methods.doc: Whether the client has completed a successful `connect`.
instance_methods.summary: Whether the client has completed a successful connect.

instance_methods.name: connection_string
instance_methods.doc: The parsed connection string the client was constructed from.
instance_methods.summary: The parsed connection string the client was constructed from.

types.name: ConnectionString
types.doc:
Common contract every database-specific connection string in CryBase
must satisfy. Subtypes provide their own `parse` and any extra fields
(ports, buckets, paths, credentials) on top of this base.

```
cs = CryBase::CouchBase::ConnectionString.parse("couchbases://h1,h2")
cs.is_a?(CryBase::Interfaces::ConnectionString) # => true
cs.hosts                                        # => ["h1", "h2"]
cs.tls                                          # => true
```
types.summary: Common contract every database-specific connection string in CryBase must satisfy.

def.name: new
def.body:
x = allocate
if x.responds_to?(:finalize)
  ::GC.add_finalizer(x)
end
x

instance_methods.name: hosts
instance_methods.doc: The cluster nodes parsed from the connection string.
instance_methods.summary: The cluster nodes parsed from the connection string.

instance_methods.name: tls
instance_methods.doc: Whether the connection should be made over TLS.
instance_methods.summary: Whether the connection should be made over TLS.

types.name: Endpoint
types.doc:
Common contract every database-specific endpoint in CryBase must
satisfy. Subtypes add their own service/role metadata and stringification
rules on top of this base.

```
ep = CryBase::CouchBase::Endpoint.new("h", 11210, CryBase::CouchBase::Service::KV, false)
ep.is_a?(CryBase::Interfaces::Endpoint) # => true
ep.host                                 # => "h"
ep.port                                 # => 11210
ep.tls                                  # => false
```
types.summary: Common contract every database-specific endpoint in CryBase must satisfy.

def.name: new
def.body:
x = allocate
if x.responds_to?(:finalize)
  ::GC.add_finalizer(x)
end
x

instance_methods.doc: The hostname or IP address for this endpoint.
instance_methods.summary: The hostname or IP address for this endpoint.

instance_methods.name: port
instance_methods.doc: The TCP port for this endpoint.

instance_methods.name: tls
instance_methods.doc: Whether this endpoint speaks TLS.
